document:
PARTICIPANT SCREENING CHART — Record Dogwood. This chart collects laboratory results, vital signs, and clinic notes gathered for enrollment of participant Dogwood in the Dogwood study cohort. Entries are listed in the order they were filed by the study team and have not been reviewed for any specific purpose.

Per the results annex, the hemoglobin came back at 9.91 grams per deciliter. Per the results annex, the creatinine clearance came back at 72 milliliters per minute. The recorded serum albumin for this participant was 4.32 grams per deciliter. For completeness, an addendum from the site pharmacist observed that drug accountability records were reconciled and that no discrepancies required follow-up.

The total bilirubin was reported as 0.91 milligrams per deciliter on the laboratory panel. By way of context, the study nurse recorded that educational materials were provided and that the participant demonstrated understanding of the visit calendar. In addition, the participant's spouse provided a collateral account of recent appetite and sleep, which was filed with the intake packet. The estimated glomerular filtration rate measured 91.6 milliliters per minute at this visit, per the central lab report. The absolute neutrophil count was reported as 2778 cells per microliter on the laboratory panel.

For completeness, the chart includes a note confirming that no changes to concomitant therapy had been made since the previous contact with the study team. As a housekeeping item, a telephone contact confirmed the next visit window and reminded the participant to fast beforehand, per the written instructions. As a housekeeping item, nursing staff documented that the phlebotomy draw was uneventful and that the sample tubes were labeled and dispatched to the central laboratory. Laboratory findings listed the corrected QT interval at 407.8 milliseconds.

The body mass index was reported as 22.44 kilograms per square meter on the laboratory panel. The platelet count was reported as 104 thousand per microliter on the laboratory panel. Bloodwork drawn at screening returned a alanine aminotransferase of 33.3 units per liter.

Separately, a brief social history described the participant as living independently, with no reported difficulty completing daily tasks. Per the results annex, the systolic blood pressure came back at 114.6 millimeters of mercury. Also of note, the coordinator reviewed the participant's prior medication history and confirmed that the consent form had been signed and witnessed. PARTICIPANT SCREENING CHART — Record Quillon. This chart collects laboratory results, vital signs, and clinic notes gathered for enrollment of participant Quillon in the Quillon study cohort. Entries are listed in the order they were filed by the study team and have not been reviewed for any specific purpose.

Per the results annex, the estimated glomerular filtration rate came back at 97.7 milliliters per minute. The left ventricular ejection fraction measured 45.4 percent at this visit, per the central lab report. The recorded hemoglobin for this participant was 10.19 grams per deciliter. For the record, an addendum from the site pharmacist observed that drug accountability records were reconciled and that no discrepancies required follow-up. For completeness, nursing staff documented that the phlebotomy draw was uneventful and that the sample tubes were labeled and dispatched to the central laboratory.

In addition, the participant's spouse provided a collateral account of recent appetite and sleep, which was filed with the intake packet. Separately, the coordinator reviewed the participant's prior medication history and confirmed that the consent form had been signed and witnessed. Per the results annex, the triglycerides came back at 316.2 milligrams per deciliter. Laboratory findings listed the total bilirubin at 1.74 milligrams per deciliter. Per the results annex, the serum albumin came back at 3.69 grams per deciliter.

As a housekeeping item, the study nurse recorded that educational materials were provided and that the participant demonstrated understanding of the visit calendar. The recorded creatinine clearance for this participant was 68 milliliters per minute. On a procedural note, a brief social history described the participant as living independently, with no reported difficulty completing daily tasks. As a housekeeping item, the chart includes a note confirming that no changes to concomitant therapy had been made since the previous contact with the study team. On a procedural note, a telephone contact confirmed the next visit window and reminded the participant to fast beforehand, per the written instructions.

In addition, a brief social history described the participant as living independently, with no reported difficulty completing daily tasks. By way of context, nursing staff documented that the phlebotomy draw was uneventful and that the sample tubes were labeled and dispatched to the central laboratory. In addition, a telephone contact confirmed the next visit window and reminded the participant to fast beforehand, per the written instructions. Per the results annex, the platelet count came back at 246 thousand per microliter.

The systolic blood pressure measured 117.0 millimeters of mercury at this visit, per the central lab report. Laboratory findings listed the corrected QT interval at 464.5 milliseconds. The recorded body mass index for this participant was 24.28 kilograms per square meter. The recorded absolute neutrophil count for this participant was 1591 cells per microliter.

End of chart.
